title: Getting-started docs still teach the retired `fab_get_rule_sections` flow
description: `docs/getting-started.md` still says the new flow uses only `fab_plan_context` + `fab_get_rule_sections`, and even instructs callers to request legacy sections like `MISSION_STATEMENT`. The live server now advertises `fab_recall` as the preferred entry point and registers `fab_get_knowledge_sections` instead of `fab_get_rule_sections`, so a fresh user following this guide will call a non-existent tool and learn an obsolete section-based contract. Similar stale naming also appears in `docs/mcp-contracts.md:21`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE_DECISIONS.md:34`, and `docs/test-seed/server.md:11`.
fix_direction: Rewrite onboarding and MCP contract docs around the current retrieval contract: prefer `fab_recall(paths)`; document `fab_plan_context` + `fab_get_knowledge_sections` only as the fallback two-step path; remove section-name examples tied to the retired rule-section API.

title: Knowledge lifecycle docs still publish pre-migration maturity names and doctor flag
description: `docs/knowledge-types.md` still describes the lifecycle as `draft / endorsed / stable` and says demotion/archive happen via `doctor --apply-lint`, but the shared contract has already locked the canonical maturity enum to `draft / verified / proven`, and the CLI exposes `--fix-knowledge` instead of `--apply-lint`. This leaves readers with an obsolete state vocabulary and a stale repair command while the codebase has moved on. The same old flag name is still repeated in `docs/configuration.md:289` and `docs/roadmap.md:49`.
fix_direction: Align lifecycle docs with the current schema and CLI: rename maturity stages to `draft / verified / proven`, update transition examples, and replace user-facing `--apply-lint` guidance with `fabric doctor --fix-knowledge` wherever the text is not explicitly documenting legacy compatibility.

title: CLI README still advertises the removed `fabric serve` command as the quickstart path
description: The package README tells users to start `fabric serve` and says the public CLI surface still includes `serve`, but the main command registry has already quarantined `serve` and no longer wires it into the shipped CLI. Following the README now sends users to a command that the current main-line binary does not expose, which is a false backward-compat promise and an onboarding dead end.
fix_direction: Update the CLI README to the stdio-only flow: `fabric install`, then validate the MCP server through the client integration rather than a local `serve` process; remove `serve` from the advertised public surface and point any historical HTTP guidance at the quarantine README if needed.

title: Canonical bootstrap still tells users to use `doctor --fix` for pending-entry review
description: The canonical bootstrap template says the 4-step loop ends with `fabric-review` or `fabric doctor --fix` to review/archive pending knowledge, and `docs/USER-QUICKSTART.md` repeats the same guidance. But the current CLI help defines `--fix` as derived-state repair only and `--fix-knowledge` as the knowledge-mutation surface. That means the compatibility wording in the bootstrap now teaches a no-op/partial remediation path right where new users learn the workflow. Related existing issue: ISS-20260531-022.
fix_direction: Change bootstrap and quickstart guidance so pending knowledge triage points to `fabric-review` and, where a CLI fallback is desired, `fabric doctor --fix-knowledge`; reserve `fabric doctor --fix` for derived-state reconciliation messaging only.
